subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics rebuilt from independent sources (IMF Art IV 2025, World Bank, V-Dem, RSF, TI, Freedom House, HRW, Amnesty, CPJ); Algerian state/state-aligned media EXCLUDED there, with economic figures used where IMF-validated.
The military ('le pouvoir') is the dominant real power; the civilian president is a figurehead.
Standout drags: a near-total hydrocarbon rentier, military-dominated institutions, suppressed liberties, migrant expulsions, and severe water/heat exposure; a relative strength is low violent crime.

economic.notes: A near-total hydrocarbon rentier with a pro-cyclical deficit, a state-controlled currency, and a shallow banking sector.

fiscal_state.notes: A hydrocarbon rentier whose deficit blew out to ~14% of GDP in 2024 on falling revenue and a rising wage bill, cushioned by a large import-cover reserve buffer that is projected to erode. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: No real central-bank independence, with a managed dinar and a large parallel-market gap. (IMF; Financial Afrik)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The dinar is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Eased markedly (~2%) after prior high years. (World Bank; IMF)

banking_stability.notes: State-dominated and shallow, with high NPLs and crowded-out private credit. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A major gas exporter (a key European supplier) but a ~95%-hydrocarbon rentier with failed diversification and a statist economy - a classic resource curse. (IMF)

institutional.notes: Military-dominated, opaque, and corruption-ridden, with a poor and worsening press environment.

rule_of_law.notes: Military-dominated, with weak judicial independence, opaque decision-making, and rampant corruption. (Freedom House)

statistical_integrity.notes: Opaque and state-controlled, with the IMF reconstructing the fiscals. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Bloated, statist, and oil-funded, with bureaucratic obstacles. (Freedom House; IMF)

press_freedom.notes: RSF ~139/180 and falling, with journalists detained and a Hirak-era crackdown. (RSF; CPJ; Amnesty)

political_social.notes: Suppressed stability and restricted liberties, with migrant expulsions and a youth strain.

political_stability.notes: A military-backed power structure with a figurehead civilian president (re-elected 2024 on low turnout) and the 2019 Hirak movement suppressed - managed/suppressed stability. (Freedom House)

civil_liberties.notes: Restricted, with curbs on protest, association, and press and prosecutions of social-media users. (Freedom House; HRW)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A transit country with mass desert expulsions of sub-Saharan migrants, racial profiling, and no real route. (HRW; Amnesty)

social_cohesion.notes: Managed, with Amazigh/Kabyle tensions and youth alienation. (Freedom House)

demographics.notes: Young and fast-growing - a severe jobs/housing/water strain with high youth unemployment. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A transactional, Russia-arms-dependent hedger with an acute Morocco rivalry and capital controls.

alliance_reliability.notes: Non-aligned and Russia-arms-dependent with an anti-Western tilt, yet a key EU gas supplier - transactional and hedged. (ICG)

conflict_involvement.notes: No direct war but an acute Morocco rivalry (severed ties, a closed border) and Sahel/Mali spillover. (ICG)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Unsanctioned but with heavy FX/capital controls and a statist economy. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Severe water/heat and wildfire exposure, against relatively low violent crime.

climate.notes: Severe water stress, extreme heat, Saharan aridity, but oil-funded readiness above the Sahel. (ND-GAIN; WB MENA)

healthcare.notes: A state system that is uneven and underfunded relative to oil wealth. (Freedom House)

infrastructure.notes: Oil-funded but inefficient, with housing shortages and water-cut protests. (IEA; Euronews)

crime_safety.notes: Relatively low violent crime - a relative strength. (independent)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low penetration against seismic (north) and flood exposure. (ThinkHazard)

summary: A near-total hydrocarbon rentier - a major European gas supplier but with ~95% of exports from oil and gas, failed diversification, and a statist economy - whose 2024 deficit blew out on falling revenue, cushioned by an eroding reserve buffer. The military dominates a corruption-ridden, opaque state with a figurehead president, suppressed liberties after the crushed Hirak movement, and documented mass expulsions of sub-Saharan migrants. Severe water scarcity, extreme heat, and recurrent wildfires weigh on the physical side, partly offset by relatively low violent crime.
